dtx_list.description: Digital therapeutic delivering Rhythmic Auditory Stimulation (RAS) using metronome/music cueing plus structured gait training and home exercise programs intended to improve gait speed/stride length, reduce freezing of gait, and support functional mobility for people with Parkinson’s disease.

dtx_list.dtx_name: Beats Medical Stroke Rehabilitation (RAS-based home therapy program)
dtx_list.description: Digital therapeutic program applying rhythmic cueing (RAS) and guided home-based rehabilitation exercises aimed at improving post-stroke walking ability and functional mobility; positioned as a stroke neurorehabilitation intervention delivered via software.

dtx_list.dtx_name: Embr Wave (companion mobile app for Embr Wave / Embr Wave 2 wearable)
dtx_list.description: Mobile software used to deliver and personalize evidence-informed thermal interventions via the Embr Wave wearable (cooling/warming pulses applied to the wrist). Therapeutic intent includes on-demand and scheduled cooling for hot-flash symptom relief and comfort modulation; user-selected programs can also be used for relaxation/stress reduction and sleep comfort by modulating perceived temperature and autonomic arousal through peripheral thermoreceptor stimulation.

dtx_list.dtx_name: Wellpepper (Patient App / Digital Care Plan App)
dtx_list.description: Patient-facing digital therapeutic app used to deliver prescribed, evidence-based care plans (commonly musculoskeletal and post-operative rehabilitation). Provides therapeutic intervention through guided home exercise programs (HEP) with multimedia instructions, scheduled tasks, adherence reminders, symptom/pain check-ins, patient education, secure messaging with the care team, and progression/escalation based on reported status—aimed at improving functional recovery and reducing complications/readmissions.

dtx_list.dtx_name: Wellpepper Digital Care Plan Platform (Clinician + Patient Web/Mobile Workflow)
dtx_list.description: Clinician-configurable digital therapeutics platform for creating and prescribing personalized care plans and remotely delivering/monitoring therapeutic interventions. Typically used for rehabilitation and chronic condition pathways, enabling protocol libraries, patient-reported outcomes capture, automated reminders and tasking, secure communication, and analytics to adjust therapy intensity and content over time.

Prescription immersive VR-based digital therapeutic for chronic lower back pain delivering an 8-week skills-based program (e.g., pain education, cognitive-behavioral and self-regulation techniques, relaxation/mindfulness) intended to reduce pain intensity and pain interference and improve function.

Legacy/previous product name for AppliedVR’s FDA-authorized prescription VR digital therapeutic program for chronic lower back pain; delivers an at-home multiweek immersive program teaching behavioral skills (e.g., CBT-informed coping skills, relaxation, mindfulness) to reduce pain and improve daily functioning. Public-facing materials commonly reference FDA De Novo authorization under this name prior to rebranding to RelieVRx.

Immersive VR therapeutic experience library used in healthcare settings for relaxation, distraction, and anxiety/pain reduction (adjunctive, non-pharmacologic). Typically positioned for acute pain/anxiety relief and patient comfort during care episodes rather than as a prescription chronic-condition program.

Software-based vision therapy for amblyopia using binocular/dichoptic training (typically with red–green/anaglyph glasses) and game-like visual tasks that adapt stimulus contrast/difficulty to promote use of the amblyopic eye and improve binocular vision; intended to be used as a structured therapeutic training program with progress tracking.

Prescription digital therapeutic delivering an evidence-based behavioral intervention (CBT-based modules, skill-building lessons, and contingency management via reinforcement) intended to increase retention in outpatient therapy and support abstinence for patients with substance use disorder (non-opioid) as an adjunct to clinician-delivered treatment.

Prescription digital therapeutic delivering a structured CBT-based therapeutic program (lessons, tools, and reinforcement/contingency management) to treat opioid use disorder as an adjunct to outpatient treatment that includes buprenorphine and clinician-delivered care; designed to improve treatment adherence/retention and reduce opioid misuse.

Prescription digital therapeutic that provides an automated cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) program (sleep restriction, stimulus control, cognitive restructuring, sleep hygiene education, and structured exercises) to treat chronic insomnia, intended to improve insomnia symptoms and sleep-related daytime impairment.

Digital musculoskeletal (MSK) physical therapy program that delivers therapeutic exercise plans and rehabilitation via a mobile app paired with motion-tracking (wearables/phone camera depending on program configuration) and clinician oversight; provides guided exercise sessions, real-time movement feedback, progressive care plans, education, and remote monitoring to treat MSK pain and functional limitations.

Digital pelvic health therapy program providing guided pelvic floor muscle training, symptom-specific exercise therapy, education, and clinician support for pelvic floor conditions (e.g., urinary incontinence, pelvic pain) and life-stage care (e.g., pregnancy/postpartum). Delivered through the Sword mobile app with structured therapeutic sessions and progress-based plan adjustments.

Digital surgery and recovery rehabilitation program focused on prehabilitation and post-operative physical therapy (commonly for orthopedic procedures). Delivers structured therapeutic exercise progression, education, and remote clinical monitoring through the Sword app to improve function and recovery outcomes around surgery.

Risk identification and proactive MSK care-routing capability intended to identify individuals at risk of MSK episodes and connect them to early intervention/preventive therapeutic care (often routing into Sword Move). Functions as a digital prevention/early-intervention component rather than a standalone consumer app.

Prescription digital therapeutic that delivers individualized rhythmic auditory stimulation (music/sound cues) synchronized to a patient’s gait during structured walking sessions, intended to drive neuroplastic changes and improve gait/walking ability after stroke. The software adapts tempo and cueing based on performance over time and is used as a therapeutic intervention rather than simple tracking.

dtx_list.dtx_name: Riva (Hypertension Management Program / Riva Health App)
dtx_list.description: Mobile-based hypertension management program that combines structured home blood pressure measurement, longitudinal BP trend feedback, behavioral/lifestyle coaching (e.g., diet/exercise adherence support), reminders, and asynchronous communication with a clinical care team to support treatment intensification and sustained blood pressure control beyond passive monitoring.

dtx_list.dtx_name: Relivion MG (Relivion wearable + companion mobile app)
dtx_list.description: Prescription, non-invasive multi-channel neuromodulation system for acute treatment of migraine. The wearable headset targets both occipital and trigeminal nerve branches (simultaneous stimulation) and is operated via a companion smartphone app that initiates/controls stimulation sessions (intensity, timing) as part of the therapeutic intervention.

Digital therapeutic for COPD self-management and home-based pulmonary rehabilitation, typically including structured PR exercise sessions, education modules, inhaler technique training, symptom monitoring, and exacerbation/action-plan support to improve function and reduce COPD-related morbidity.

Digital therapeutic for asthma self-management, typically including personalized education, inhaler technique support, symptom and peak-flow monitoring, trigger awareness, and action-plan guidance to improve control and reduce exacerbations.

dtx_list.dtx_name: Aduro (Aduro mobile app / Whole Health platform)
dtx_list.description: Digital behavior-change intervention delivered via a member mobile experience plus remote coaching and personalized programs. Typically includes structured lifestyle content, goal-setting, habit formation, check-ins, and coaching workflows intended to improve health behaviors (nutrition, activity, stress, sleep) and reduce cardiometabolic risk.

Digitally delivered diabetes prevention intervention aligned to lifestyle-change curricula (e.g., weight loss, nutrition, physical activity, self-monitoring) with coaching and ongoing engagement features, designed to prevent/delay progression from prediabetes to type 2 diabetes.

A digital mental health intervention that combines a mobile app with continuous physiological sensing (via a companion wearable) to detect emotional arousal/stress patterns and deliver in-the-moment coping interventions (e.g., guided breathing/relaxation, skills prompts) alongside longer-term behavioral support (e.g., self-awareness, mood/emotion regulation routines).

Game-based biofeedback digital therapeutic for children that uses a heart-rate sensor to detect physiological arousal and dynamically adjust gameplay; children practice evidence-based calming and emotional regulation skills (e.g., down-regulation/breathing/relaxation strategies) to progress through games, supported by parent coaching content to generalize skills to real-world situations.
